SuperOPCWatchIt.notes:
# SuperOPCWatchIt — release notes

## v3

**Standalone OPC-DA — nothing to install, no "Class not registered".**

- Replaced the OpenOPC + `OPC.Automation` wrapper stack with a vendored native
  32-bit helper (`opcda-bridge.exe`) that talks the OPC-DA custom interfaces
  directly. The tool no longer needs any OPC automation wrapper (Graybox /
  Matrikon / `gbda_aut.dll`) or any COM registration — the only requirement is
  the OPC server itself (e.g. `OPC.DeltaV.1`), which is already present on a
  DeltaV node.
- Fixes the "Connection error / Dispatch: Class not registered" failure, which
  was caused by the missing automation-wrapper COM class.
- Clearer connection errors: the status line now reports the actual OPC/COM
  reason (e.g. server ProgID not registered, item not writeable, unknown item).

*Note:* runs on the DeltaV node itself (local COM). Connecting to a remote
node's OPC server over DCOM is not enabled in this build.

## v2

- Advanced OPC-DA tag monitor: live browsing, CSV import/export, write audit log.

DvTwin.notes:
## v18

- Fixed a startup crash that prevented the app from launching. After the shared FHX parser was consolidated, the FHX simulation generator constructed parser objects with an incompatible constructor, raising `TypeError: Element.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'classifier'` at import time. The shared `Element` now accepts those keyword arguments and the app launches again.

ABComms.notes:
## v4

- Restored the EDS-based chassis catalog lookup. The shared Allen-Bradley EDS library was missing from the packaged build, so catalog-number resolution silently failed; it is now bundled and working again.

Op2Live.notes:
# Op2Live

## v14
- **Control-valve "top oval" — and every other chord shape — now converts.** The GRF
  scanner didn't recognise the iFix *Chord* primitive, so it was silently dropped before
  conversion: valve-actuator ovals (and any other chord) went missing. Chords now convert
  (a full-circle chord renders as an ellipse at the same box), recovering the shape on every
  affected symbol. Class-wide — it applies to every picture, not one valve.
- **Conversion rules generalised — no site-specific names hard-coded.** A sweep replaced
  name-matching heuristics with behaviour-based rules that read the same signals in *any*
  DeltaV project, so the tool isn't tuned to one site's naming:
  - Tag-label widening keys on the label's structure (a Tag-bound auto-size field), not a
    "grpTags" name.
  - Mode/status read-out widening skips conditionally-hidden overlays (interlock / hold
    badges) by their visibility, not a "lock" name.
  - The redundant faceplate close-button is dropped by what it *does* (invokes ClosePicture),
    not by its name — so a differently-named close button is still handled.
  - Faceplate-vs-detail classification uses the DeltaV framework lifecycle calls first, using
    the `_fp`/`_dt` convention only as a last resort when there's no other evidence.
  - Out-of-box picture skipping is driven purely by the OOB catalog cross-reference.
- **Analog eng-units read-outs no longer show "**".** An indicator whose units were a typed-in
  caption (no live units source) inherited a sibling's live-units binding, whose path didn't
  resolve. Static-units indicators now keep their literal caption.

## v13
- Runtime render fixes from a live V953 import of the v12 conversion, plus more GRF-engine
  parity against the Operate-export golden.
  - **Analog indicators no longer show "???????".** A value-symbol GEM (the OUT.CV / eng-units
    read-out) declared only its module Tag, not the DataSource it actually binds, so the value
    links resolved to nothing at runtime. The GEM now declares a DataSource whenever its body
    uses one — every analog-link value read-out (e.g. LI-36406) resolves. Class-wide.
  - **Valves no longer mis-colour (green where they should read white).** Device symbols were
    clustered by glyph shape only, so two geometrically-identical valves with *inverted* state→
    colour tables collapsed into one variant and one inherited the other's colours. Clustering
    now also fingerprints each device's colour convention, so opposite-convention valves keep
    their own tables.
  - **Blinking alarms no longer hidden behind a "checkerboard".** An iFix cross-hatch alarm
    overlay was converted with an opaque background, painting a solid box over the blinking
    indicator beneath it. Hatch/pattern fills now convert with a transparent background (only
    the pattern lines paint), matching Operate — the blink shows through.
  - **DO state words no longer clipped ("Disabled" → "Disa…").** The v12 widen pass ran before
    the state-word source was finalised, so it missed the word links. It now runs after, sizing
    the box to the full word. (Completes the v12 attempt.)
  - **Fewer runtime "bad status" errors on interlock badges.** A bypass-indicator whose source
    was runtime-driven (empty in the export) left a dangling animation name that DeltaV flagged
    as bad status; it now defaults to hidden, as in Operate.
- GRF-engine golden-parity improvements (Operate-export fidelity):
  - **Correct analog lookup value types.** Lookup tables were typed by the first animation that
    shared a name, so reused animation names cross-contaminated their input types; each lookup
    is now typed from its own data.
  - **DeltaV ActiveX controls (alarm summary, tab strips, prompt lists) emit only their real
    property set** instead of a generic control-property block.
  - **Faithful graphic thumbnails.** Preview thumbnails now render from the source graphic
    (composite pump/valve symbols draw their real geometr

Patina.notes:
# Patina v3

- **Fixed: Batch (and single decompiles) timing out on large binaries.** A
  binary containing one giant basic block — common in obfuscated code, huge
  compiler-generated dispatch routines, or a misidentified code/data region —
  could spin for many minutes in the IR optimizer, blow past the per-target
  batch timeout, and be killed with *no output at all*. The within-block
  inline/dead-code pass was super-linear in the block's size; it's now bounded so
  a single pathological block can no longer stall a run. Such blocks still
  decompile (in the existing flat "fast mode" view) instead of being dropped.
  Example: a 5 MB bridge binary that never finished now completes in ~30s, and a
  whole folder of them decompiles with zero timeouts.

# Patina v2

- **Fixed: Decompile and Batch did nothing but reopen the window.** Clicking
  Decompile (or Batch, or Auto-fix) relaunched the Patina window instead of
  running the decompiler, because the shipped build looked for a separate
  `patina.exe` next to itself and — on Windows' case-insensitive filesystem —
  matched its own `Patina.exe`. Patina is now a single self-contained binary:
  the same exe is both the GUI and the decompiler it runs, so Decompile/Batch/
  Auto-fix work in the installed build.

# Patina v1

- First release inside the CornerBench suite (Reverse Engineering category).
- Decompiles Windows PE and Linux ELF binaries to readable C via an x86
  disassembler (iced-x86) and a custom IR.
- Function navigation with cross-references.

_Edit this when you bump the version; the launcher shows it as "What's new"
after an in-place update._

EDSCatalog.notes:
## v2

- Fixed a startup crash (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'shared.allenbradley'`). The shared Allen-Bradley EDS library was missing from the packaged build; it is now bundled correctly and the tool launches again.

ABCommission.notes:
## v5

- **Probe now prefers the DPI object and reads cleanly on PowerFlex 520-series
  drives.** Validated against a real PowerFlex 525:
  - *Auto* now uses the DPI Parameter Object (0x93) when the device answers a full
    record — it returns name, units, min/max and scaling in one read (e.g.
    parameter 1 → name "Output Freq", units "Hz").
  - The standard Parameter Object (0x0F) fallback was hardened for the PF525's
    quirks: it recovers the parameter name when the drive returns it in the units
    attribute, and **infers the value type from its byte length** when the drive
    reports a non-standard type code — so values show as numbers instead of raw hex.
- Enumeration no longer depends on a working parameter-count read (walks until the
  device stops answering), and the object scan reports parameter-count correctly.

## v4

- **Device introspection — no PDF/EDS needed for most devices.** New *Device
  Explorer* tab plus a *Probe device* button on the parameters tab. The tool now
  asks the device to describe itself:
  - **Identity** — reads the Identity Object (0x01) for product name, revision,
    vendor/type/code, serial.
  - **Self-describing parameters** — walks the DPI Parameter Object (0x93) or the
    standard Parameter Object (0x0F) and reads each parameter's name, type, units,
    min/max/default and **scaling** straight from the device, so you get a live,
    named parameter list (with engineering-unit values) without an EDS. If an EDS
    *is* loaded it enriches the device data (help text, enum strings).
  - **Object-model scan** — probes the well-known CIP classes and shows which the
    device actually implements.
  - **EDS-off-device** (experimental) — uploads the device's own EDS via the File
    Object (0x37), the way Studio 5000/RSLinx do.
  - **Generic explicit-message poker** — send any service/class/instance/attribute
    and decode the reply, for anything the built-in views don't cover.
- **Fixed a second latent read bug:** responses were parsed from the start of the
  raw reply frame instead of the CIP data offset (byte 44). Values/IP config are now
  extracted correctly, and CIP error statuses are decoded to plain English.

## v3

- **Fixed "no parameters could be read" on drives/adapters.** Explicit-message
  reads and writes were being wrapped in an Unconnected_Send routed across a
  backplane to "slot 0" — correct for a ControlLogix chassis, but a standalone
  EtherNet/IP device (PowerFlex drive, EtherNet/IP adapter) has no backplane and
  rejects that route, so every read failed. The tool now sends bare UCMM explicit
  messages directly to the device, the way RSLogix and the drive AOP do.
- **Added the DPI Parameter Object (class 0x93)** used by PowerFlex 520-series
  drives (single read via attr 9; write to NVS via attr 9 or RAM-only via attr 10),
  alongside the generic Parameter Object (0x0F). The Device Parameters tab now has
  a **Parameter object** selector (Auto / DPI 0x93 / Standard 0x0F, default Auto,
  which tries DPI then falls back to 0x0F) and a **Write to** selector (NVS
  permanent / RAM-only temporary).

## v2

- Fixed a startup crash caused by the shared Allen-Bradley EDS library being missing from the packaged build. EDS-based parameter lookup and the tool as a whole now launch correctly.

OperateTools.notes:
# OperateTools

## v9
- **Corrected release.** v8 shipped with an invalid tamper signature that made the
  updated exe fail its launch integrity check ("This copy … has been modified and
  won't run"). v9 is the same code as v8, re-signed correctly (Authenticode + the
  Ed25519 tamper signature). No functional changes vs v8.

## v8
- **Adding a term to an expression no longer gets silently skipped over spacing.**
  A replacement that glued a parenthesis or comparison onto a tag (e.g.
  `…DISC_ALM.F_ENAB)` or `…F_NALM>0` instead of `…F_ENAB )` / `…F_NALM > 0`) was
  reported as an un-appliable *whole-expression rewrite*. The replacement is now
  normalized to iFix's one-space-per-token form before editing, so these apply as
  a clean add-a-term. Operand tags, the `-`/`/` characters, and quoted string
  literals are left untouched.
- **Clearer reason when a replacement is skipped.** Unbalanced parentheses — the
  most common typo — are now called out specifically ("Parentheses are unmatched
  — the replacement has 1 more ')' than '('…") instead of a generic message, and
  an unbalanced replacement is refused rather than compiled into a broken
  expression.
- **Warns about the `$1_TE_01` capture-group trap.** In a regex replacement,
  `$1_TE_01` is read as the group *named* `1_TE_01` (which blanks out), not group
  1 followed by `_TE_01`. Find & Replace now flags this the moment it appears and
  tells you to use `${1}` instead.

## v7
- **Validation now shows *what* it flagged, not just a count.** After a
  find/replace, each output-file warning/error is listed in full under its file
  (e.g. `validate: 0 err / 1 warn` now spells out the actual message), and the
  messages are also written into the exported audit CSV as `validation` rows — so
  a change-control record captures exactly what the validator said.
- **Removed a false-alarm warning.** The offset-table "out-of-order entries"
  warning fired on every expression edit because the editor appends its rewritten
  records to the end of the file (which iFix loads fine — it indexes by table
  position, not file order). It no longer reports this benign case, so a clean
  expression edit now validates `0 err / 0 warn`.
- **Bigger tables are easier to read.** The Find & Replace results table and the
  Reports table now scroll horizontally as well as vertically, columns are
  resizable (drag a header border), and any clipped cell shows its full text on
  hover — so nothing is hidden on wide/long tables.

## v6
- **Whole-expression replacement now works on every expression, including ones it
  previously skipped.** Some animation expressions are stored by iFix in a second
  internal form (the evaluation/RPN token stream kept as a separate copy rather
  than shared with the display tokens). v5 could not safely rewrite those and
  reported them as *skipped*; they had to be re-entered by hand. v6 rebuilds that
  form to the standard shared form as part of the replacement, so a whole-
  expression rewrite applies to them automatically — no manual editing. Matches
  that genuinely can't be rebuilt are still reported honestly as skipped.

## v5
- **Fixed the "Include expressions" toggle in Find & Replace, which behaved
  backwards.** Previously, leaving it unchecked would still silently overwrite
  animation expression text (desyncing it from the underlying logic iFix
  evaluates), while checking it made expression matches vanish from the preview.
  Now: unchecked leaves every expression untouched and edits only plain
  captions/values; checked edits expressions through the structure-preserving
  engine. Matches it can't safely apply are clearly reported as *skipped* (with a
  reason) instead of being dropped or corrupted. The preview now exactly mirrors
  what an apply will do.
- **New: whole-expression replacement.** Find & Replace can now replace an entire
  animation expression with different text — not just rename tags/values/operators
  or add/remove a term. The expression's token gra

VIMTools.notes:
# VIMTools v1

First release. Reverse-engineered toolkit for Emerson/Mynah DeltaV **VIM**
(Virtual I/O Module) configuration files.

- **Open `.vio` configs** — browse the VIM → DeltaV controller → data-set tree,
  with EtherNet/IP device definitions and channel mappings.
- **EtherNet/IP mapping view** — see how assembly buffer bytes map to DeltaV
  signals (`Bytes I4-5 → PCS1-CTL57010101`, etc.).
- **Read-only diagnostics** — connect to a live VIM and observe its status/frames
  (never writes, flashes, or reboots).
- **File validation** — rejects non-`.vio`/corrupt files instead of silently
  showing an empty result.

Clean-room implementation; no Emerson software required.

CornerRDP.notes:
# CornerRDP

## v1
- First release. A deterministic RDP console for reaching Windows / DeltaV / OT
  nodes that `mstsc` can connect to but WinRM cannot — Standard RDP Security (RC4),
  TLS, and NLA — via the vendored `rdpilot` engine.
- Live desktop with click-to-click control and typing, session clipboard,
  file push/pull, run-as-SYSTEM commands, guest-agent deploy, and reboot.
- Connections are saved as reusable profiles; passwords are never persisted.
